input.background: Children out of School in America is a report on delinquency of school-age children prepared by the Children's Defense Fund (CDF), a government-sponsored organization. The report includes three reported results based on surveys conducted in specific regions. The details of the reported results and the corresponding facts are provided.
input.question: Analyze Reported result 2: Of all the secondary-school students who had been suspended more than once in census tract 22 in Columbia, South Carolina, $33 \%$ had been suspended two times and $67 \%$ had been suspended three or more times. Fact: CDF found only three children in that entire census tract who had been suspended; one child was suspended twice and the other two children, three or more times. Identify the potential distortions in this result.

output.answer: The reporting of $33 \%$ and $67 \%$ is misleading because it is based on an extremely small sample size of only three children. Using percentages instead of the actual numbers (1 child suspended twice and 2 children suspended three or more times) can exaggerate the significance of the data. The small sample size makes the results unreliable for generalization.

meta_info.caption: More Misleading Descriptive Statistics- Delinquent Children
meta_info.chapter: Methods for Describing Sets of Data
meta_info.section: 2.10 Distorting the Truth with Descriptive Techniques
